label: Sage Intacct
description: Connect to Sage Intacct's XML Web Services API using multi-credential authentication to access financial data, GL entries, invoices, and more.

form.title: Connect Sage Intacct
form.subtitle: Provide your Sage Intacct sender credentials and company login to authenticate via the XML Web Services API.

methods.label: XML Web Services Credentials
methods.description: Paste your Sage Intacct sender ID, sender password, company ID, user ID, and user password. This is the standard multi-credential authentication Sage Intacct requires for all API integrations.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Sage Intacct XML Web Services

Sage Intacct uses a two-layer credential system: **sender credentials** (identify your integration application) and **company/user credentials** (identify who you are within Intacct). Both layers are required for every API call.

### Step 1 — Obtain Sender Credentials

1. Log in to the [Sage Intacct Marketplace / Developer Portal](https://developer.intacct.com/) or contact Sage Intacct support to request a **Sender ID** and **Sender Password** for your integration.
2. If you are an existing Sage Intacct partner or have a licensed Web Services subscription, your Sender ID and password will have been issued when you enrolled. Check your original onboarding email or ask your Sage account manager.
3. Keep the Sender Password secure — it is tied to your integration application, not your personal account.

### Step 2 — Enable Web Services for Your Company

1. Log in to your Sage Intacct company as an administrator.
2. Go to **Company → Admin → Subscriptions** and confirm that **Web Services** is enabled.
3. Go to **Company → Setup → Company** → click the **Security** tab.
4. Under **Web Services authorizations**, click **Add** and enter your **Sender ID** to whitelist it for your company.

### Step 3 — Identify Your Company and User Credentials

1. Your **Company ID** is shown on the **Company → Setup → Company** page (or in the URL after login).
2. The **User ID** and **User Password** are the credentials of a Sage Intacct user account that has the API permissions you need. It is best practice to create a dedicated Web Services user:
   - Go to **Company → Admin → Users → Add**.
   - Set the user type to **Business** or **Web Services only**.
   - Assign the appropriate roles/permissions for the data Anton needs to access.

### Notes

- All five credentials are required — the API will reject requests missing any of them.
- The sender credentials are shared across companies; the company/user credentials are company-specific.
- If you manage multiple Sage Intacct entities, you may need to specify a **Location ID** or use entity-level credentials depending on your setup.
- Rotate the Web Services user password in Intacct and update it here if you ever suspect exposure.

fields.name: sender_id
fields.label: Sender ID
fields.description: The Sender ID issued by Sage Intacct for your integration application. Found in your developer enrollment or onboarding email.

fields.name: sender_password
fields.label: Sender Password
fields.description: The password paired with your Sender ID. Issued alongside the Sender ID by Sage Intacct.

fields.name: company_id
fields.label: Company ID
fields.description: Your Sage Intacct company identifier. Found under Company → Setup → Company, or visible in your login URL.

fields.name: user_id
fields.label: User ID
fields.description: The Sage Intacct user account Anton will authenticate as. A dedicated Web Services user is recommended.

fields.name: user_password
fields.label: User Password
fields.description: Password for the Sage Intacct user account specified above.

fields.name: entity_id
fields.label: Entity / Location ID (optional)
fields.description: For multi-entity companies, specify the entity or location ID Anton should operate against. Leave blank to use the top-level company context.

fields.name: endpoint_url
fields.label: API Endpoint URL
fields.description: Sage Intacct XML gateway URL. Leave as default unless your Sage account uses a custom endpoint.
